description: IB VI, 22b. Letter from Alexandria sent around 1120 CE from a Maghribi merchant, Makhlūf b. Musa al-Nafusi (Ibn) al-Yatim, who proclaims that a friend carried for him a purse with 101 “fresh” (‘tari’) dinars weighing 100 and one/fourth. He asks the addressee to “sell” them, meaning to convert the Sicilian currency he was dispatching into local Egyptian money. Makhlūf devotes eleven lines of the short letter to an apology for not buying his friend a Sicilian turban that was ordered. Confirming the reason by two oaths with God’s great name, the writer acknowledges the omission was due to his own forgetfulness. (S. D. Goitein, Mediterranean Society, 1:237, 459; 5:248, 575 ) EMS
description_he: מכתב מאלכסנדריה, כ-1120, מסוחר מגרבי מח'לוף בן מוסא אל-נפוסי: שולח ארנק עם 101 דינר טרי ומבקש להמירם לכסף מצרי מקומי. מתנצל בי"א שורות על שכחתו לקנות כובע סיציליאני שהוזמן.
